item.title: A 21st century solution to my own housing crisis.

item.title: Eilev Bråtene
item.description: A Norwegian storyteller born in 1828, Bø in Telemark. Described as a very short hunched man with round glasses in front of intelligent eyes. He used an axe as a walking stick, a rucksack that always had stuff hanging out of it and held a . He hardly ever bathed, but he was welcomed by everyone as they knew him as a captivating storyteller. According to himself he had met both Jutul (a type of giant) and the hulder who tried to seduce him. He married a widow 24 years older than him, who was known for her physical strength. On the day of their wedding, she that carried him over a river they had to cross to get to the church, to the amusement of the locals. Some of his stories was documented by Ingebret Moltke Moe, son of Jørgen Moe who gathered stories that you might know like "Three Billy Goats Gruff" and "Chicken Little". Eilev Bråtene died in 1899 when the 70 year old lost his footing and fell into a river. The newspaper obituary described him as an (roughly translated) "old weirdo".
item.dc:creator: TheAnswerWasAlwaysMoreLube

item.title: Hands Out Of Your Pockets For Officer Safety
item.dc:creator: BenDoverTeeHeeHee

item.title: How We Blocked a Coal Plant and Got a Solar Plant, Instead
item.description: I don't know who needs to hear this, but: A few years ago, I moved my family to a rural valley in the Western US. Beautiful. I found out a few weeks later that there were plans for a company from Las Vegas to build an enormous coal-fired power plant here, with 400-foot tall smokestacks, and all the massive pollution that'd come with it. I get the need for electricity, but it was a poor choice of location. I asked the Vegas ppl, off the record, why here? They said the rest of the state already violated clean air laws. Experts predicted a loss of 6-12 years of life for every resident if it was built. So, I started a website, email newsletter, got local cable TV access for broadcasting public hearings, and set up a magazine to get the word out that not everyone was okay with that. Others agreed. One local lady set up a voting initiative that power plants would need voters' approval. And because the group had no money to speak of, a couple of non-lawyer friends put a court challenge together. The case went to the state supreme court. We won. It turned out that the only evidence of research for the permit to pollute our valley was a scribbled approval on a post-it note. Some locals complained the local government had lost out on tax revenues. But a few months later, a solar power station was proposed by another company. That's part of it, in the photo. It's silent, clean, peaceful -- and everyone is living 6-12 years longer. Not all superheroes wear capes. Don't let people, including wealthy people, push you around. Save your worlds, my dudes, and you help save the whole world. (Below is what that area would have looked like, had the coal-fired power plant been built.)
item.dc:creator: MeanwhileInAmerica

item.title: This is Zoe Thorogood, she's an author and artist
item.description:
I've been following her for a while and love her stuff.
Here is some of the stuff she has worked on IT’S LONELY AT THE CENTRE OF THE EARTH - JOE HILL’S RAIN - LIFE IS STRANGE - HACK/SLASH - HARLEY QUINN - CREEPSHOW
She along with several other artists recently had a problem with the art company that was repping them. See what's currently known of the story here, https://www.comicsbeat.com/what-is-happening-at-cadence-comic-art/ . Likely more details will come out over time. I figured I'd do what I could to support her during this difficult time, and support any imgurians looking for some cool comics to check out, by sharing some of her work here.
Check out more of her work here https://www.instagram.com/zoethorogood
item.dc:creator: DropDrop

item.title: Do not skip the Primaries...
item.description: Yes, everyone is gearing up for Novembers vote, but do not skip the primaries. The city I live in has 3 ballot measures coming next month, and one of them would allow the city to demand voter identification (a tactic commonly used to disenfranchise minorities) and allow the city to post "monitors" at polling places and ballot drop-off boxes. Yes, these would obviously be overturned in court as unconstitutional, but it means that for the Nov 2024 election they would have an opportunity to have these weekend warriors with their weapons on display demanding voters show ID before allowing them to drop off a ballot or go into a voting booth. Do not skip the primaries, this is where the GOP is laying groundwork to challenge the November election. FP Edit - Don't need any pics, but if you want to see what measures or local races might be hidden in your primary check out Ballotpedia's sample ballot system. enter your address, don't bother giving them an email, and they will show you what you have the right to vote on. - https://ballotpedia.org/wiki/index.php?title=Sample_Ballot_Lookup&Source=sidebar
item.dc:creator: blacksheep214

item.title: Scarlet Witch cosplay by me. I won the event!
item.description:
Sharing fantastic photos from the recently held Popcon Festival in Dubai! I wore the Scarlet Witch costume for the first time at the event and won a prize. Now I'm set to go to Portugal to represent the UAE on GCC. It's a very responsible and exciting opportunity!
Dancing with my photographer as a bonus))
item.dc:creator: pufacosplay
